title: Bracteola subaequigluma
intro: Bracteola subaequigluma, also known as Cuatle or Cuatele, is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Bracteola subaequigluma include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as aucubin, which are responsible for its pharmacological effects. In traditional medicine, Bracteola subaequigluma is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which are the most common herbal preparations made with this plant.
